title: 11 Proven Herbal Tinctures to Combat Coughing Fits

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Cichoric acid is good for coughing fits because it helps to reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, allowing for easier breathing and relief from persistent coughing.
Quercetin is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat, making it easier to breathe and alleviating coughing spasms.

Alkylamides are good for coughing fits because they have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the irritated airways and throat, reducing the frequency and severity of coughing episodes.
Polyphenols are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the irritated tissues in the throat, reducing the severity and frequency of coughing.
Flavonoids are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and relax.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures are often used to help with coughing fits because of its natural properties. This plant, also known as coneflower, has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to fight off infections and boost the immune system. The tincture is made by mixing the plant's roots and flowers with a liquid, usually alcohol or glycerin, which helps to extract its active ingredients. These ingredients, including alkylamides and caffeic acid, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. When taken orally, the tincture can help to reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and swallow. This can be especially helpful for people with conditions like bronchitis or pneumonia, where coughing fits can be a major symptom. Additionally, the tincture may also help to stimulate the body's natural defense mechanisms, making it easier to fight off the underlying infection that's causing the cough.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hydrastis canadensis herbal tincture for coughing fits, first, you will need fresh or dried Goldenseal roots. If using fresh roots, clean and dry them thoroughly, then chop them into small pieces. If using dried roots, simply chop them into smaller pieces. Next, combine the chopped roots with a high-proof grain alcohol, such as vodka, in a clean glass jar. A general rule of thumb is to use a 1:5 ratio of roots to alcohol, for example, one part Goldenseal roots to five parts vodka. Secure the jar with a lid and let it sit in a cool, dark place for several weeks, shaking the jar every few days, allowing the roots to extract their medicinal properties into the alcohol. After four to six weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. The resulting liquid tincture can be taken orally, a few drops at a time, as needed, to help soothe and alleviate coughing fits.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal tinctures for coughing fits

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin in <built-in method capitalize of str object at 0x7660f2791c70> Hydrastis canadensis is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated throats, reducing congestion and coughing.
Saponins are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated throats and airways, reducing the frequency and severity of coughs.
Flavonoids help to relieve coughing fits by reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, which can help to calm a sore throat and make breathing easier.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture, also known as licorice root tincture, is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, which can cause coughing. Licorice root tincture also has a soothing effect on the mucous membranes in the throat. When we have a cough, the mucous membranes can get dry and irritated, leading to more coughing. The licorice root tincture helps to keep these membranes moist and calm, which can stop the coughing fit. Additionally, licorice root tincture has expectorant properties, which means it can help bring up mucus and phlegm from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and get rid of. This can help relieve congestion and coughing fits.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hydrastis canadensis herbal tincture that helps with coughing fits, start by gathering the necessary ingredients, including dried Goldenseal root, a high-proof spirit like vodka or glycerin, and a clean glass jar with a lid. Begin by measuring out one part of dried Goldenseal root to two parts of the spirit in the glass jar, making sure the root is completely covered. Next, seal the jar and place it in a cool, dark place, allowing it to steep for two to three weeks, shaking the jar occasionally. This process is called maceration, and it allows the active compounds in the Goldenseal root to dissolve into the spirit. After the steeping period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. You can now use the tincture as needed to help soothe coughing fits, typically by taking a few drops in water or another liquid. It's essential to note that Goldenseal tincture should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional, as it can interact with certain medications and have side effects in some individuals.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tinctures for coughing fits

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Thymol is good for coughing fits because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the throat and lungs, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by coughing.

Carvacrol is good for coughing fits because it has natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the throat and fight off underlying infections causing the cough.
Rosmarinic acid is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm irritated airways, reducing the severity and frequency of coughs.
Tannins are good for coughing fits because they help soothe and calm the throat, reducing inflammation and killing bacteria that can cause coughing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris tincture is made from thyme, a herb known for its soothing effects on the respiratory system. It has been used for centuries to calm coughs and relieve congestion. The active compounds in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have antimicrobial properties that help to fight off infections that can cause coughing fits. When taken as a tincture, thyme can help to reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe. It can also help to loosen and clear mucus, giving the body a chance to recover from illness. Thyme tincture is especially helpful for people with bronchitis, pneumonia, or other respiratory infections that cause persistent coughing.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Hydrastis canadensis tincture to help with coughing fits, you will need dried and powdered goldenseal root, a clean glass jar with a lid, a solvent like vodka or glycerin, and a strainer. First, measure out one part of the powdered goldenseal root and mix it with one part of the solvent in the glass jar. Make sure the solvent completely covers the goldenseal root. Then, seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for two to three weeks. During this time, the solvent will extract the medicinal properties from the goldenseal root. After the two to three weeks, use the strainer to separate the liquid from the goldenseal root. Discard the solid goldenseal root, but keep the liquid tincture. Transfer the tincture to a smaller glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. To use the tincture, take a few drops in water or as directed by a healthcare professional, and it may help to soothe and calm coughing fits. Remember to follow proper safety guidelines when handling herbal remedies and consult a healthcare professional before using them, especially if you have any health conditions or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus tincture is made from the leaves and flowers of the mullein plant. The plant has been used for centuries to help with coughing fits, especially at night. This is because the tincture has a soothing effect on the throat and airways, reducing inflammation and calming the cough reflex. The active compounds in Verbascum thapsus, such as saponins and flavonoids, help to break down mucus and phlegm, making it easier to cough up. This can be especially helpful for people with respiratory conditions like bronchitis or asthma, where coughing fits can be a major symptom. When taken as a tincture, the mullein plant's properties can help to quiet a persistent cough, allowing for a restful night's sleep.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hydrastis canadensis tincture that helps with coughing fits, you'll need some dried Hydrastis canadensis roots, a clean glass jar with a lid, a bottle of high-proof vodka or glycerin, and a cheesecloth or coffee filter. First, chop the dried roots into small pieces and place them in the glass jar. Then, pour the vodka or glycerin over the roots, making sure they are completely covered. Next, seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the roots release their medicine into the liquid. After the steeping time, strain the liquid through the cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean bottle, discarding the solids. This liquid is your Hydrastis canadensis tincture, which can be taken as needed to help soothe coughing fits. It's recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, and always consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Cineole is good for coughing fits because it has natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the irritated airways, reducing the severity of coughs and promoting a healthy recovery.
Eucalyptol is good for coughing fits because it has strong soothing properties that help calm and clear congested airways, making it easier to breathe.
Tannins are good for coughing fits because they have antitussive properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, providing relief from persistent and irritating coughs.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tincture is a popular remedy for coughing fits because of its natural decongestant properties. It is made from the leaves of the eucalyptus tree, which contain a compound called eucalyptol. This compound helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. When we have a cold or flu, our airways can become congested with mucus, leading to a persistent cough. The eucalyptol in Eucalyptus globulus tincture helps to break down this mucus, allowing us to cough it up more easily. This can provide quick relief from a coughing fit, especially if it is caused by excess mucus. Additionally, the tincture has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe the throat and reduce discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hydrastis canadensis herbal tincture for coughing fits, first, you'll need dried goldenseal root, a glass jar, and a liquid base like vodka or glycerin. Start by measuring out a cup of dried goldenseal root and placing it into the glass jar. Next, pour 2 cups of the liquid base over the goldenseal, making sure the root is completely covered. Now, seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar gently every few days to help the herbs infuse into the liquid. After the steeping time, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, pressing on the solids to get as much liquid out as possible. Discard the solids and store the goldenseal tincture in a cool, dark place. To use the tincture, take 20 to 30 drops in water 3 times a day as needed to help soothe coughing fits.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, is a natural remedy that can help with coughing fits. The essential oil in peppermint has a cooling effect on the throat, which can help to soothe and calm it. When you're having a coughing fit, the muscles in your throat are tight and sore, making it hard to breathe. The menthol in peppermint oil helps to relax these muscles, allowing them to unwind and relax. This relaxation helps to reduce the severity of the cough, making it easier to breathe and feel more comfortable. Additionally, the menthol in peppermint oil also helps to thin and loosen mucus in the throat, making it easier to expel. This can be especially helpful during colds and flu when mucus build-up can exacerbate coughing fits. By using a peppermint tincture, you can apply the cooling and relaxing properties of peppermint directly to your throat, providing quick and effective relief from coughing fits.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hydrastis canadensis herbal tincture for cough relief, start by gathering fresh or dried goldenseal roots. Clean the roots thoroughly with water to remove any dirt or debris. Next, combine one part of the cleaned goldenseal roots with two parts of a solvent, such as 80-proof vodka or glycerin, in a clean glass jar. The ratio can be adjusted as per preference, but one part herb to two parts solvent is a good starting point. Close the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar well every day to help the solvent extract the active compounds from the goldenseal roots. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Transfer the liquid to smaller dropper bottles and store them in the refrigerator to slow down the extraction process. Hydrastis canadensis tincture is traditionally used to soothe coughs and relieve respiratory issues, but consult a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tincture, also known as ginger tincture, is often used to help with coughing fits. Ginger contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties. When you have a cough, it can cause your airways to become inflamed, making it harder to breathe. The anti-inflammatory compounds in ginger tincture can help to reduce this inflammation, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of your cough. Ginger tincture also has expectorant properties, which means it can help to bring up mucus and other fluids that may be causing your cough. This can help to clear your airways and reduce the frequency and severity of coughing fits. Additionally, ginger tincture has natural soothing properties that can help to calm your throat and reduce irritation, making it a popular natural remedy for coughs.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hydrastis canadensis herbal tinctures that help with coughing fits, start by gathering high-quality dried roots of goldenseal, which is the botanical name for Hydrastis canadensis. First, fill a clean glass jar about one-quarter full with the dried goldenseal roots. Next, pour a high-proof neutral-tasting spirit, such as vodka or rum, over the roots, making sure they are completely covered. Seal the jar tightly, label it, and store it in a cool, dark place for at least two to three weeks, shaking the jar gently every day to help the spirit extract the active compounds from the roots. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Finally, store the goldenseal tincture in a cool, dark place and use it as needed to help soothe and calm coughing fits, typically taking 10-20 drops of the tincture in water three to four times a day.

These properties help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and lungs, making it easier to breathe and swallow. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hydrastis canadensis herbal tincture, also known as goldenseal root tincture, which helps with coughing fits, you will need to gather the root of the plant and dry it properly. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Hydrastis canadensis tincture that helps with coughing fits, start by gathering the necessary ingredients, including dried goldenseal roots, a glass jar with a lid, a measuring cup, and a carrier oil like glycerin or vodka. intro_desc: If you're experiencing persistent and painful coughing fits, herbal tinctures can provide relief. One effective combination is Echinacea purpurea and Glycyrrhiza glabra. Echinacea purpurea is an immune system booster that helps reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, making it easier to breathe. This herb has natural antiviral and antibacterial properties that fight off infections and promote healing. On the other hand, Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the throat and calm coughing fits. It also has a natural expectorant effect, helping to bring up mucus and phlegm from the lungs.  Coughing fits can disrupt your daily life, making it difficult to sleep, eat, and even breathe. If left untreated, this can lead to complications like bronchitis, pneumonia, or even heart problems. By addressing the underlying issue, you can improve your quality of life and regain your energy. Drinking herbal tinctures made with Echinacea purpurea and Glycyrrhiza glabra can help reduce the frequency and severity of coughing fits, allowing you to sleep better, eat more comfortably, and engage in physical activities without discomfort. By taking control of your health, you can enjoy everyday activities without being held back by persistent coughing.
supplementary_best: A combination of Eucalyptus globulus, Thymus vulgaris, and Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures can help remedy coughing fits. Eucalyptus globulus tincture contains eucalyptol, an expectorant that loosens and clears mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up. Thymus vulgaris tincture has thymol, an antimicrobial agent that fights off infections causing the cough. Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture contains glycyrrhizin, an anti-inflammatory that soothes the irritated mucous membranes and reduces coughing. This combination works well because the expectorant in Eucalyptus globulus helps clear the mucus, while the antimicrobial agent in Thymus vulgaris prevents the infection from spreading. Meanwhile, the anti-inflammatory in Glycyrrhiza glabra reduces the irritation and helps the mucous membranes heal, reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits.
supplementary_avoid: If you have coughing fits, there are some herbal tinctures you should avoid. One such herb is Aconitum napellus, also known as Monk's Hood. This herb can be toxic and may cause more harm than good. It's used to treat fever and pain, but its potency can be too much for a person with a cough. Consuming it can lead to heart problems and even death.  Another herb to be cautious of is Arum maculatum, or Cuckoo Pint. Its roots contain toxic compounds that can cause nausea, vomiting, and even paralysis if ingested in large amounts. While it's sometimes used to treat fever and rheumatism, its side effects can worsen a coughing fit.  You should also avoid Digitalis purpurea, or Foxglove, if you have a cough. Its leaves contain a toxic compound that can cause irregular heartbeat and even death if consumed in large amounts. While it's used to treat heart problems, its potency can be too much for a person with a cough.  Lastly, avoid Heracleum mantegazzianum, or Giant Hogweed, if you have a cough. Its sap contains toxic compounds that can cause skin irritation, blisters, and even kidney damage if ingested. While it's sometimes used to treat skin conditions, its side effects can worsen a coughing fit.  It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional or a qualified herbalist before using any herbal tincture, especially if you have a cough or other health conditions. They can help you choose a safe and effective herbal remedy for your needs.
supplementary_other_remedies: Honey can help soothe a cough. It's thick and sticky texture coats the throat, reducing irritation and discomfort. When you cough, the muscles in your throat contract and this can cause further irritation, but honey's soothing properties can calm these muscles and help you feel better. Honey also has antimicrobial properties, which can help fight off infections that may be causing your cough.  Slippery elm is another natural remedy that can help with coughing fits. The inner bark of the slippery elm tree contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in your throat. When you cough, this mucilage can help calm and protect your throat, reducing irritation and discomfort. Slippery elm is also anti-inflammatory, which means it can help reduce swelling in the throat and make it easier to breathe.  Licorice root has been used for centuries to soothe coughs. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the throat and make it easier to breathe. Licorice root also has a thick, protective coating that can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in your throat, reducing irritation and discomfort.  Gargling with warm salt water can also help soothe a cough. The salt helps reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the throat, making it easier to breathe. The warm water also helps loosen mucus and make it easier to expel.  Steam inhalation can also help relieve a cough. When you inhale steam, it helps loosen mucus and make it easier to expel. You can inhale steam from a bowl of hot water or a steam humidifier. Adding eucalyptus oil or menthol to the steam can help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the throat.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have coughing fits may also experience other health issues that can be helped with medicinal herbs. One common problem is bronchitis, an inflammation of the airways that can cause a persistent cough. The herb Thyme (Thymus vulgaris) is often used to treat bronchitis because it has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight off infections. Another issue is asthma, a condition where the airways become narrow and swollen, causing wheezing and coughing. The herb Echinacea (Echinacea spp.) is sometimes used to help manage asthma symptoms because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the airways. People with coughing fits may also experience sinus infections, which can cause headaches, facial pain, and a yellowish discharge from the nose. The herb Eucalyptus (Eucalyptus globulus) is often used to help relieve sinus congestion because its essential oils can help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages. Furthermore, people with coughing fits may also experience pneumonia, a serious infection of the lungs that can cause fever, chills, and difficulty breathing. The herb Licorice root (Glycyrrhiza glabra) is sometimes used to help soothe and calm the lungs, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
